label: Prioritize features
description: Data-driven feature prioritization and RICE scoring

options.label: Generate RICE scores
options.prompt: Help me RICE score a set of feature requests. I'll list the features — for each one, help me estimate Reach, Impact, Confidence, and Effort. Calculate the RICE score and rank them. Challenge my assumptions where the data suggests a different estimate.

options.label: Cross-reference requests with usage data
options.prompt: Help me decide what to build next. I'll describe the feature requests — cross-reference them with what we know about user behavior, drop-off points, and support tickets. Recommend which features would have the highest impact on retention and activation.

options.label: Map trade-offs between options
options.prompt: I have several features competing for the same engineering time. Help me map the trade-offs: user impact vs effort, short-term vs long-term value, revenue impact vs retention impact. Present a recommendation with clear reasoning.

label: Write a PRD
description: Product requirements documents from research and data

options.label: Full PRD from feature description
options.prompt: Write a PRD for the feature I'll describe. Include: problem statement (with data), user stories, success metrics, requirements (must-have, nice-to-have, out of scope), technical considerations, design requirements, launch plan, and open questions. Keep it concise — PMs read these in 5 minutes.

options.label: One-page brief
options.prompt: Write a one-page product brief for the feature I'll describe. Include: the problem (1 paragraph), the proposed solution (1 paragraph), success metrics (3-5 bullets), key risks (2-3 bullets), and estimated timeline. This is for exec review — be crisp.

options.label: Generate user stories
options.prompt: Generate user stories for the feature I'll describe. Use the format: 'As a [user type], I want to [action] so that [benefit].' Include acceptance criteria for each story and group them into logical epics. Prioritize by user value.

label: Summarize this sprint
description: Sprint summaries with shipped features and metrics

options.label: What did we ship?
options.prompt: Summarize what the team shipped this sprint. Pull merged PRs and completed tickets, group them by feature area, and write a summary suitable for sharing with the broader team. Highlight user-facing changes vs infrastructure work.

options.label: Plan vs actual
options.prompt: Compare what we planned for this sprint vs what we actually completed. List: completed items, items carried over, unplanned work that came in, and anything descoped. Calculate our completion rate and suggest adjustments for next sprint planning.

options.label: Cross-functional update
options.prompt: Generate a cross-functional sprint update for design, engineering, and stakeholders. For each audience: highlight what's relevant to them, flag any dependencies or blockers they should know about, and preview what's coming next sprint.

options.label: User-facing release notes
options.prompt: Draft user-facing release notes from recent merged PRs and completed tickets. Write in a friendly, non-technical tone. Group by: New Features, Improvements, and Bug Fixes. Include screenshots or descriptions of UI changes where relevant.

options.label: Technical changelog
options.prompt: Generate a technical changelog from recent commits and PRs. Include: breaking changes (with migration guide), new APIs, deprecations, performance improvements, and bug fixes with issue references. Follow Keep a Changelog format.

options.label: Release announcement email
options.prompt: Draft a release announcement email for customers. Lead with the most exciting feature, include 2-3 supporting improvements, add a CTA to try the new features, and keep it under 300 words. Write in our brand voice — helpful, not salesy.

label: Analyze feature adoption
description: Feature adoption funnels and drop-off analysis

options.label: Adoption funnel for a feature
options.prompt: Help me analyze the adoption funnel for a feature I'll describe. Break it into stages: aware → tried → adopted → retained. For each stage, suggest what metrics to track, where the likely drop-offs are, and what might improve conversion.

options.label: Cohort retention analysis
options.prompt: Help me set up a cohort retention analysis for a feature. I'll describe the feature and user base — suggest: cohort definitions, time windows, retention events, and how to segment by user type. Draft the analysis query if possible.

options.label: Design an A/B test
options.prompt: Help me design an A/B test for a feature change I'll describe. Include: hypothesis, primary and secondary metrics, sample size calculation, test duration estimate, segmentation strategy, and guardrail metrics to watch for negative effects.
